floor time is best time
Janeway has done some shit.
Newspapers: UGH, WHY AREN'T PEOPLE RETURNING TO THE OFFICE Workers: Because working from home is much less stressful and more helpful than working from the office? Newspapers: BUT OUR OWNERS HAVE MONEY INVESTED IN OFFICE SPACE >:(
This is actually possible now :)
FOR THE EMPRA
Donkey Kong has depression
Concern
me
For Chats: http://discord.abbii.gay For Shitposts: http://tumblr.abbii.gay For srs and maybe shitposts: http://mastodon.abbii.gay
item.dc:creator: abbiistabbii

item.title: From the Lancet ...
item.dc:creator: blank666man

item.title: The end of a very long search.
item.description: There was a song on a random VHS we rented back in the early 80's. Years went by and I couldn't remember the name of the film (I was a kid). I thought I found it once in the 90's, playing in a shop in Dublin, but it was on a ripped CD with no track listing. Figured it would eventually pop up again one day, but was always a bit sad it was so elusive. Finally, Blue In Green by Miles Davis played on the radio this morning. I leapt out of bed like a madman, to Shazam the shit out of it, ending a tortuous 40 year search. Happy New Year to you all! https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=TLDflhhdPCg&ab_channel=MilesDavisVEVO Edit: Ooh, this has gone well. If you can help anyone in the comments find their lost 'thing' it would be great to spread the joy. Thanks to @AliceHope78 for suggesting a subreddit called 'tip of the tongue', it might bear fruit if you're looking for something difficult.
item.dc:creator: TedBuckley

item.title: Welp, it's the final unhinged XL CeleryDump of the year (for the good & bad people of Imgur)
item.description:
greetings online friends. just want to say thanks to everyone here for being so kind and supportive of my posts this year. if you are a regular commenter- you are the coolest, in my book. blah blah here's some memes
slappa da bass
better yet, a dog president
every time
what a beautiful painting
those crafty goats
nope
dang that's sick
mmm hmm
couldn't be anything else
even better than goblin mode
you only really need the ice cream option though
accurate
i would say the flute is the least scary instrument
that won't be necessary. this is a real album btw
yes
no one else!
that’s it for me.. have a great 2024 gang
item.dc:creator: CeleryMan7

item.title: 2024… let’s keep this gains train going
item.description: 2021 to 2023. It’s been nonlinear, but I have learned so much from strength training, injury, rehab, and rest. Going to apply those lessons to 2024. Gain with me.
item.dc:creator: magpieinthemirror

item.title: Happy new year from Bulgaria
item.dc:creator: fromtheabyss

item.title: Happy New Year, Imgur, and thanks for all the fish!
item.description:
(artist: nekosnicker) To all of you who shitpost, comment, and lurk, happy new year and thank you from me and Story. This year had its ups and downs at Elbow House, more recently being a rapid series of downs that you folks helped me navigate. I thought the breakdown of my car would be the worst of it, but then my little furgoober got sick and everything felt small by comparison.
Before this past November, I'd never experienced an animal emergency where I didn't have a dozen other coworkers, supervisor, and on-call veterinarian to lean on. Raising horses on a farm is nothing like raising a pet alone. I learned a lot, real quick, and not in the way I wanted. Watching my cat go from active and attention seeking to lethargic and practically immobile in the span of a few hours was one of the worst fears I've experienced in a long, long time. I lose track of how many of you reached out to talk to me while Story was hospitalized two hours away, and kept in touch after she got back home. You people kept me sane so I could keep focused on caring for my furball.
Her recovery was slow, which isn't a pace I usually operate at. Everything I had going on - projects, restorations, socializing, recreation - got put on hold while I worked with Story on taking her medication, adjusting the house to suit her restrictions, and just being around when she wanted company.
The morning I woke up to this Ring notification, I finally let myself relax a little. Something as simple as seeing my cat drinking water without being coaxed into it was huge. It's like holding your breath snd finally being allowed to exhale.
We made slow adjustments to accommodate her arthritis, made the living room *her* room, and anything else I could think of to make her feel comfortable.
It took over a month of trying one prescription food after another, trying out different ways to serve it, different bowls, different locations, all while Story's stomach was upset from the sudden diet change to dissolve the stones in her bladder and clear out her crystals. She wasn't happy about the checkup, but she bounced back quick.
Now that her stones are gone, she was able to reacclimate to her original diet until her tummy settled and is now transitioning slowly onto her prescription food.
It took a lot of time, stress, and minor emotional breakdowns, but Story's back to her spunky 10 year old self!
It goes without saying, but thank you to everyone who helped pull my fat out of the fire over the past few months. Between the car repair bills and Story's medical expenses, I'd be deep in debt were it not for the kindness of so many friends and strangers. You guys were the difference that kept a financial gutshot from turning into a financial landmine.
But more importantly, you folks took away a massive weight of stress at a time when I was having trouble enough with seeing my baby girl in pain. Story's doing much better now, thanks in part to all of you who kept me sane during the holiday season. 2023 was a rough one, and I have high hopes that 2024 will be a little bit better with this fuzzy screwball keeping me company.
(artist: inowiseei) Thank you to all of you who tolerate my weird brand of shitposting, the occasional antique restorations, and glimpses of the real me. Story thanks you too. I'll see you weirdos next year. <3
item.dc:creator: ElbowDeepInAHorse

item.title: Queen Margaret of Denmark abdicates after 52 years on the throne.
item.description: Our queen, one of the oldest reigning Monarchs in the world will abdicate in favour of her son, January 14th, 52 years after she ascended to the throne. She will be missed as she holds her last New Years speech to the Danes.
item.dc:creator: Cebrail

item.title: Happy New Year to me!
item.description: Honestly, feeling pretty damned good! I'm down from nearly 400lbs to 300lbs in just over 8 months total. I now need to look forward to getting back into the gym, getting a proper workout routine and all that fun stuff. God, I can't believe how many clothes I've had to donate! I've got a suit in my closet that I forgot I even owned. I've had it since I was 21 years old, I'm 37 now, and for the first time since I was 21? It fit! Like...Wow!! Feelin pretty damned stoked. Though, I -definitely- am going to need help figuring out workouts and such. Been a long time since I've been in the gym due to injuries and other social reasons (videos being taken of me for youtube 'make fun of the fat guy' purposes...Not fun) So...any motivation and the like would be greatly appreciated! Though for now? Happy new years from Ontario Canada! T-minus 4 hours and 56 minutes to midnight! Edit: Here is a link to the 'before/after' pics for those who were wondering. Mature because well, boxers and shirtless! https://imgur.com/gallery/5YQExu1
item.dc:creator: DraccoKnightblade
